cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this date problem. So today is the second day of the third month of 1966. That means today is March 2nd, 1966. But in this case, since the problem is about calendar months, I think it's safer to assume that it's referring to the same day of the previous month. So March 2nd minus one month is February 2nd. But wait, but if I think about how many days in February in 1966? Because 1966 is not a leap year. Leap years are divisible by 4, but 1966 isn't. 1964 was a leap year, 1968 is next. So that seems conflicting. Because 30 days before March 2nd is February 1st. But if we go back one month, it's February 2nd. So which one is correct? Ah, the problem says "a month ago".
